summary: High‑quality soundboard recording of a quintessential ’76 Boston run with superb Scarlet, St. Stephen, Dancin’ and Sugar Mag → Eyes sequences
review: Fans rave about the fluid, laid‑back groove of this June 1976 Boston show, highlighting scorching performances of Scarlet Begonias, St. Stephen, Dancin’, Sugaree and the magical Eyes → Stella Blue transition. The soundboard source (often called a “Betty Board”) is praised as clear and crisp despite occasional channel‑swap quirks, making the music shine.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews, most 4‑5 stars, repeatedly cite outstanding playing and excellent soundboard quality; only minor technical complaints (channel swap, tiny cuts) keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, Betty‑Board style mix praised as “incredible”; a few reviewers note left/right channel swap (piano/Mickey on opposite sides) and occasional tiny cuts, but overall clear and balanced.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows with a relaxed ’76 groove, moving from the tight funk of Scarlet into a soaring St. Stephen jam, then a flawless Dancin’ transition into Music and the second‑set highlight of Sugar Mag → Eyes → Stella Blue.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers praise Garcia’s stripped‑down solos, jaw‑dropping runs on St. Stephen and Samson, and his mellow tone throughout.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s descending bass line in Samson and the “mad” scarlet runs are singled out as highlights; his bass is described as “sick” and “mad”.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s piano shines on the Eyes → Stella Blue transition, adding a lush layer to the climax.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey’s cowbell and drumming are noted (channel‑swap comment); his playing is described as fluid, weaving with Bill Kreutzmann’s beats.
